title: Orange & Ginger Rye Scones
description: Earthy rye scones flavored with fresh orange zest and grated ginger, finished with a professional turbinado sugar crust.
ingredients: 1/2 cup (1 stick) Unsalted Butter, cold and cubed
1/2 cup Dried Cranberries OR Macadamia Nuts
steps: Freeze the Butter: For the flakiest scones, cube your butter and put it in the freezer for 10 minutes before starting. Keeping the fat solid is the secret to scone texture.
Mix Dry Ingredients: In a large bowl, whisk together the AP flour, rye flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt until perfectly combined.

Incorporate Butter: Add the frozen butter cubes to the flour. Use a pastry cutter or your fingertips to rub the butter into the flour until it looks like coarse crumbs with some pea-sized chunks remaining.
Add Aromatics: Stir in the orange zest, grated ginger, and your choice of dried cranberries or chopped macadamia nuts.
Whisk Wet Ingredients: In a separate small bowl, whisk together the heavy cream, egg, vanilla, and orange juice.
Combine: Pour the wet ingredients into the dry. Stir with a fork until a shaggy dough forms. Do not overwork! If it's too dry, add a teaspoon more cream.
Shape: Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface. Gently pat it into an 8-inch disc (about 1 inch thick).
Cut: Use a sharp knife to cut the disc into 8 wedges. Place them on a parchment-lined baking sheet, spaced 2 inches apart.
Professional Finish: Brush the tops with the extra tablespoon of cream and sprinkle generously with Turbinado sugar to create that crunchy, professional crust.
Bake: Bake at 400°F for 18–22 minutes until the edges are golden brown.
notes: Cold is Key: If your kitchen is warm, pop the shaped scones in the fridge for 15 minutes before baking. For a glaze upgrade, whisk together powdered sugar and orange juice to drizzle over cooled scones.

title: Banana Oat Power Muffins
description: A sophisticated, bakery-style evolution of the classic breakfast bar using Rye Flour and Sour Cream for moisture.
ingredients: Topping: 1/2 cup oats, 1/4 tsp cinnamon, 2 tbsp brown sugar, 2 tbsp melted butter
steps: Prepare the Oven: Preheat to 375°F. Line a 12-cup muffin tin or grease thoroughly with butter or oil.
Whisk Wet Ingredients: In a large mixing bowl, combine mashed bananas, eggs, melted butter, sour cream, brown sugar, and vanilla. Whisk vigorously until smooth and well-emulsified.
Mix Dry Ingredients: In a separate medium bowl, whisk together the flours, oats, soda, baking powder, salt, and nutmeg.
Combine: Gently fold the dry ingredients into the wet mixture using a spatula. Stop mixing as soon as no dry flour streaks remain. Do not overmix, or the muffins will be tough.
Fold in Extras: Stir in the chocolate chips, macadamia nuts, and seed mix until just incorporated.
Bake: Divide the batter evenly among the 12 muffin cups. Bake for 20–22 minutes until the tops are golden brown and a toothpick comes out clean.
Cool: Allow to cool in the pan for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack.
notes: Sour Cream Secret: The acidity reacts with the baking soda to create carbon dioxide bubbles, giving these muffins a significantly better 'lift' and dome height.

title: Brown Butter & Garden Herb Chicken
description: Chicken cutlets flavored with garden herbs and a brown butter lemon sauce, served over creamy Parmesan polenta.
ingredients: Fresh Herbs: 4-5 Sage leaves, 1 sprig Rosemary, 2 sprigs Thyme, 1 sprig Oregano
steps: The Creamy Polenta: Bring 1.5 cups water/broth to a boil. Slowly whisk in polenta. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15–20 minutes. Once creamy, stir in 1/4 cup Parmesan and 1 tbsp butter.

Prepare the Herb Rub: Finely chop the Thyme and Oregano. Pat the chicken cutlets very dry. Season both sides with salt, pepper, and the chopped herbs, pressing them into the meat to ensure they stick.
The Herb-Infused Sear: Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high. Add chicken and sear for 3–4 minutes per side until golden brown. Remove to a plate and tent with foil.
Brown Butter & Crispy Herbs: Wipe the pan of burnt bits, then add 2 tbsp butter. Once it foams, add Sage leaves, Rosemary sprig, and smashed Garlic.
Deglaze: Fry for 1 minute until butter turns light amber. Immediately add lemon juice and broth to stop the browning. Scrape the pan for flavorful bits.
The Finish: Let the sauce reduce by half (about 2 minutes). Add chicken back in for 30 seconds to coat in the glossy herb butter.
Plating: Spoon polenta onto plates, top with chicken and sauce, and garnish with the crispy sage and fried rosemary.
notes: Pantry Pairing: Sauté Broccolini in the same pan with garlic and lemon after the chicken is done. Toast pine nuts or sliced almonds for an extra crunch.

title: Beef & Lentil Cottage Pie
description: Ground beef and lentil filling topped with a creamy potato mash.
ingredients: 1 lb Potatoes, peeled and cubed (any variety)
steps: Prepare the Mash: Boil cubed potatoes in salted water for 15–20 minutes. Drain. Add cream and butter, then mash until smooth and creamy.
Cook Filling: In an oven-safe skillet, brown the beef. Drain all but 1 tbsp fat. Add diced carrots and celery. Sauté for 5 minutes.
Simmer: Stir in lentils, rosemary, and thyme. Pour in 2 cups broth. Bring to a boil, then simmer covered for 20–25 minutes until lentils are soft and gravy is thick.
Assemble: Spread potato mash over the beef/lentil mixture. Use a fork to create ridges for crispy edges. Sprinkle with cheddar.
Bake: Preheat oven to 400°F. Bake for 10–15 minutes (or broil for 3–5) until the cheese is bubbly and golden brown.
Sauté Greens: Steam broccolini in 1/4 cup broth for 3 minutes, then sauté with butter and lemon once the broth evaporates.
notes: If the mixture looks dry before lentils are soft, add the remaining 1/2 cup of broth. A cast iron skillet is ideal for this recipe.

title: Crispy Miso-Ginger Tofu
description: Crispy cornstarch-coated tofu glazed in a rich miso-ginger sauce with charred vegetables over jasmine rice.
steps: Start the Rice: Rinse 1 cup Jasmine rice and cook according to package instructions.
Prep Tofu: Pat tofu cubes very dry with paper towels. Toss in a bowl with cornstarch and a pinch of salt until every side is chalky and coated.
Sear: Heat a generous splash of oil in a large skillet. Fry tofu cubes until golden and crispy on all sides (8–10 minutes). Remove and set aside.
Sauté Veggies: In the same pan, add broccolini and carrots. Add a splash of water and cover for 2 minutes to steam-soften, then uncover and sauté until lightly charred.
Glaze: Whisk all glaze ingredients in a small bowl. Lower the heat to medium.
Combine: Add tofu back to the pan. Pour the sauce over everything. Toss quickly for 30–60 seconds until the sauce thickens and glazes evenly.
Serve: Serve immediately over Jasmine rice, garnished with fresh green onions.
notes: Ensure tofu is pressed and patted very dry; moisture prevents the cornstarch from becoming truly crispy.

title: Banana Breakfast Bars
description: Hearty, nutrient-dense breakfast bars packed with oats, nuts, and fruit.
ingredients: 1/4 cup Sliced Almonds or Macadamia nuts
steps: Prep the Pan: Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a 9x13 inch pan with parchment paper and grease lightly.
Mix Dry Base: In a large bowl, combine the flours, oats, baking soda, salt, nutmeg, brown sugar, and shredded coconut.
Mix Wet Base: In a separate bowl, mash bananas until smooth. Whisk in syrup, vanilla, melted coconut oil, and eggs until well combined.
Combine: Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix until just combined.
Fold in Inclusions: Gently fold in the toasted pecans, dried berries, nuts, and the seed mix.
Bake: Spread the mixture evenly into the pan. Bake for 25–30 minutes until edges are golden brown and the center is set.
Cool: Allow to cool completely in the pan before slicing. This is essential for them to hold their shape.
notes: These freeze beautifully. Wrap individual bars in foil for a quick grab-and-go breakfast.

title: Greek Yogurt Banana Bread
description: A dense, moist loaf utilizing Greek yogurt for a tender crumb and a crunchy turbinado sugar finish.
ingredients: 1/4 tsp ground nutmeg (or a large pinch)
1/2 cup plain whole milk Greek yogurt
steps: In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking soda, salt, and nutmeg. Set aside.
In a mixer, whisk the eggs lightly until broken up.
Add the oil (or melted butter), Greek yogurt, and vanilla. Mix until combined, then add the brown sugar and mix again.
Add the mashed bananas and mix until fully incorporated.
Gently add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture, mixing only until just incorporated to avoid a tough bread.
Grease a loaf pan and line with parchment paper. Pour the batter in and smooth the top.
Bake at 350°F for 45–55 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean.
Let cool in the pan for 10–20 minutes before transferring to a rack. Sprinkle generously with turbinado sugar for a crunchy crust.
notes: Using Greek yogurt provides a significant 'lift' and ensures the bread stays moist for days. Very ripe, spotted bananas are essential for the best natural sweetness.

title: Simply Sandwich Bread
description: Soft and buttery with a chewy/crisp crust, this fresh loaf of bread is a kitchen staple.
ingredients: 3 1/3 cups (425g) AP Flour or Bread Flour
steps: Prepare the Dough: Whisk warm water, milk, yeast, and sugar in the bowl of your stand mixer. Cover and let sit for 5 minutes.
Mix: Add butter, salt, and 1 cup of flour. Beat on low using the dough hook. Add another cup and beat on medium. Add remaining flour until dough pulls away from the sides.
Knead: Knead in the mixer for 5–8 minutes (or 10 mins by hand) until the dough is soft, tacky, and bounces back when poked.
1st Rise: Place in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm spot for 1–2 hours until doubled.
Shape: Punch the dough down. Roll into an 8x15 inch rectangle. Roll it up into an 8-inch log.
2nd Rise: Place log in a greased 9x5 pan. Cover and rise until the dough is about 1 inch above the rim (about 1 hour).
Bake: Bake at 350°F on a lower rack for 32–36 minutes until golden brown. Internal temperature should be 195–200°F.
Cool: Allow to cool in the pan for a few minutes before transferring to a wire rack.
notes: Freezing: Shape the loaf and freeze in the pan for up to 3 months; thaw and rise for 5 hours before baking. Bread flour is recommended for a chewier texture.

title: Dark Chocolate Coconut Cream Cake
description: A moist, rich chocolate cake utilizing coconut cream as the primary fat source, loaded with frozen berries and topped with a decadent cocoa cream cheese frosting.
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour (plus 1 tbsp for berries)
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F and grease an 8x8 inch pan (metal is preferred for even browning).
Whisk coconut cream, sugar, egg, and vanilla until well combined.
Stir in flour, cocoa powder, baking soda, and salt until just mixed.
Toss frozen berries in 1 tbsp of flour (this prevents them from sinking and creating a soggy center), then fold them and the chips into the batter.
Bake for 45–55 minutes. Because of the frozen fruit, the center takes longer to set. Test with a skewer in the very center; it should come out with just a few moist crumbs.
Cool completely in the pan to allow the structure to finish setting.
For the frosting: Beat cream cheese and butter until smooth.
Add sugar and cocoa, mixing until fluffy. Adjust consistency with cream.
Apply half of the frosting batch to the cake (save the rest in the freezer).
notes: Using frozen fruit increases bake time significantly. If the edges brown too fast, tent with foil for the last 10 minutes. Must be refrigerated.

title: Korean Meatballs
description: Sweet, spicy, and flavorful baked meatballs made with ground beef and gochujang. Perfect for a quick weeknight dinner or meal prep.

ingredientSections.label: Garnish
items: Sesame seeds and extra green onions

ingredients: 1 tbsp low sodium soy sauce (for glaze)
Sesame seeds and extra green onions (for garnish)
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F and line two large baking sheets with foil.
In a large mixing bowl, combine all meatball ingredients until just mixed; avoid overmixing to keep meatballs tender.
Roll the mixture into 24 evenly sized meatballs (a cookie scoop helps) and place 12 on each baking sheet.
Bake for 10–15 minutes or until the internal temperature reaches 160°F.
While meatballs bake, combine glaze ingredients (brown sugar, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and gochujang) in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Whisk the glaze occasionally for about 5 minutes until thickened; do not let it boil.
Remove meatballs from the oven and brush generously with the thickened glaze.
Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced green onions if desired, and serve.
notes: Meatballs can be frozen after baking. If freezing, do not apply the glaze; let them cool completely and freeze on a tray before transferring to a bag. Glaze them after thawing and reheating. Serve with udon noodles or jasmine rice.

title: Spicy Japanese Sweet Potato & Corn Chowder
description: A creamy, spicy vegetarian chowder featuring Japanese sweet potato, fresh herbs, and jalapeño, blended to silky perfection.
ingredients: 1 Japanese Sweet Potato, cut into 1/2in cubes
4 cups Chicken Broth (from Roasted Chicken Bouillon)
steps: Melt 2 tbsp butter in a large pot over medium heat. Add onions, carrots, and jalapeño. Sauté for 6–8 minutes until onions are translucent.
Add rosemary, thyme, and sage. Stir in garlic and smoked paprika for 60 seconds until fragrant.
Sprinkle AP flour over the vegetables. Stir constantly for 2 minutes to cook out the raw flour taste.
Slowly pour in chicken broth, stirring to prevent lumps. Add the frozen corn and Japanese sweet potato cubes.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Simmer for 20–25 minutes until the sweet potatoes are perfectly tender.
Stir in heavy cream and fresh oregano. Cook on low for 5 minutes.
Use an immersion blender to blend the entire pot until smooth and creamy.
notes: The Japanese sweet potato adds a natural creaminess and subtle sweetness that balances the heat from the jalapeño. Blending the whole pot ensures a silky texture.

title: Brown Butter Satin Brownies
description: Rich, fudgy brownies with nutty brown butter and bloomed Dutch-process cocoa. A high-speed egg whip creates the signature crackly, glossy top.
ingredients: 4oz (113g) dark or semisweet chocolate (60% cacao), finely chopped
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F and line a 9x13" pan with parchment paper.
Combine: Put cocoa powder, chopped chocolate, and espresso powder in a heat-proof bowl.
Brown the Butter: Melt butter in a saucepan over medium-low. Once it foams, turns nutty, and the milk solids turn brown, remove from heat.
Bloom the Cocoa: Immediately pour the hot brown butter into the cocoa bowl. Stir until smooth and set aside to cool.
Whip the Base: In a mixer, whip sugars, vanilla, and eggs on medium-high for 4-5 minutes (for fudgy) or 8-10 minutes (for fluffier) until thick and pale.
Combine: Reduce speed to low and slowly pour the cooled chocolate-butter mixture into the egg foam until incorporated.
Fold: Using a spatula, fold in flour and salt until just incorporated. Do not overmix.
Bake: Spread batter evenly into the pan. Bake for 25 minutes until the surface is glossy and set. Do not overbake.
notes: The secret to the crackly top is the egg aeration. For extra thick brownies, use an 8x8" pan and increase bake time slightly. Let cool 30 minutes before slicing.

title: Salted Chocolate Chip Cookies
description: A perfectly balanced cookie with a hint of almond and a finishing touch of flake salt to enhance the chocolate.
steps: In a mixer, cream together the room temperature butter, white sugar, brown sugar, vanilla, and almond extract until smooth (about 3 minutes).
Add the room temperature egg and mix until fully combined.
Add the flour, baking soda, and salt. Mix on low until just combined, then fold in the chocolate chips.
Scoop the dough into balls and place on a tray. Chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes to improve texture and prevent spreading.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Arrange cookies on a baking sheet and bake for 10–12 minutes until edges are golden.
Remove from oven and immediately sprinkle with flake salt while still warm.
notes: Chilling the dough for the full 30 minutes is the secret to a thicker, chewier cookie. I leave a bag in my freezer and pull them out as needed, throw straight into hot oven.
